author: mikodin
description: Record voice notes, Fill in lost thoughts, Transcribe the audio, Summarize & Visualize the text - All in one clip

manifest.author: Mike Alicea
manifest.description: Record, transcribe, and transform voice notes into structured insights. Leverage Whisper or AssemblyAI and ChatGPT to fill in gaps, generate summaries, and visualize ideas — all seamlessly integrated within Obsidian.
manifest.name: Scribe

name: Scribe

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Scribe 2.3.1 - Feature: Enables Pause
This has been a long standing request, it's nice to finally have it in.

## What's Changed
* 51 pauze functionality request by @Mikodin in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/98


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.3.0...2.3.1
releases.name: Scribe 2.3.1 - Feature - Enables the user to pause a recording

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Scribe 2.3.0 - Bug Fix - LLM summarization completion cancelling new recording
Blocks the user from beginning a new recording when there is LLM processing happening in the background.

This is the simplest solution to this problem. 
The reality is that it would beautiful to be able to simply start more recordings.  In order to enable this, we would need to rearchitecture the app, and the changes would be much larger than I would like in order to include a queuing system of sorts.

And so, this will do it :)

## What's Changed
* 48 llm summarization complete cancels the recording by @Mikodin in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/97


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.2.9...2.3.0
releases.name: Scribe 2.3.0 - Bug Fix - LLM summarization completion cancelling new recording

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Scribe 2.2.9 - Bug Fix Appending Audio Overwriting Audio frontmatter instead of appending
This fix has two components to it
1. We are properly appending to the `audio` frontmatter instead of overwriting
2. We are writing the frontmatter immediately after audio file creation.  This enables us to not have to dig for the audio file if our transcription request fails.

## What's Changed
* 79 audio property is incorrect when using save audio append to current multiple recordings by @Mikodin in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/96


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.2.8...2.2.9
releases.name: Scribe 2.2.9 - Bug Fix Appending Audio Overwriting Audio frontmatter instead of appending

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Scribe 2.2.4
This release is solid, I tested in a wide range of scenarios, everything should be good to go.  This fixes the OpenAI Whisper issue, turns out the `fix-webm-duration` package started to break recordings that were over 30 seconds by making `audioContext.decodeAudio` throw errors.  This was reported in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/84

## I did a few things to clean up
1. Removed `fix-webm-duration`, this was the crux of the issue.  The duration was appearing fine across the board for me on MacOS and iOS
2. Implemented and used [standardized-audio-context](https://github.com/chrisguttandin/standardized-audio-context) package to remove inconsistencies (hopefully) through Webkit and Chrome, etc
3. Updated all dependencies
4. Removed MP3 options.  Sorry @tggo , it wasn't working in any scenarios (ios or macos) for me and it being a desktop only thing anyway (because of ffmpeg and the webworkers), I decided to ditch it.

## What's Changed
* 84 fix chunking, mime type encoding, removes mp3 support, standardize audio, update all deps by @Mikodin in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/89


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.2.3...2.2.4
releases.name: Scribe 2.2.4 - Bug Fixes & Dep Updates - Fully fix openAI issues

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Scribe 2.2.3 Fixes decoding failed issue with OpenAI for transcription
It appears that something changed in Safari / Webkit in how decodeAudioData works. I am not sure what it is exactly, I wasn't able to get it to work in any reliable way. For now, when this error happens, we are just passing the entire file to OpenAI

This will create a known issue with file sizes larger than 25MB not working. But it's better than any recording longer than 30 seconds not working.

I need to rework file splitting. Things have gotten wonky in how files and the audiobuffer are being dealt with, I need to unify it.

## What's Changed
* Update README.md by @bdwelle in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/87
* 84 garbage decoding always fails take2 by @Mikodin in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/88

## New Contributors
* @bdwelle made their first contribution in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/87

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.2.2...2.2.3
releases.name: Scribe 2.2.3 - Bug Fix OpenAi for transcription now works

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
Reverts releases/2.2.1

## What's Changed
* Revert "84 garbage decoding always fails" - issue is still happening, I went on the wrong trail by @Mikodin in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/86


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.2.1...2.2.2
releases.name: Scribe 2.2.2 Revert Bug Fix - Issue persists and may break other users experience

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Scribe 2.2.1 Bug Fix - Removes mp3 option due to it not working on iOS or MacOS
Selecting MP3 was introduced several months ago in PR https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/53 unfortunately it wasn't working as expected and when I merged it, I didn't test it thoroughly.  

At first glance it appears to work, but on recordings more than 30 or so seconds, it fails when sending to OpenAI.  I also had conversion fail on both MacOS and iOS, so it was always falling back to WebM anyway.

## What's Changed
* 84 garbage decoding always fails by @Mikodin in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/85


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.2.0...2.2.1
releases.name: Scribe 2.2.1 Bug Fix - Removes mp3 option due to it not working on iOS or MacOS

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description: # Scribe 2.2.0 This version was pushed through by @skorphil as he pushes to enable us to support the Gemini API's, he figured he may as well overhaul the settings to make the environment easier to work in. The result is a beautiful settings pane with tabs for each section. The code is much cleaner as well, he overhauled how we add settings making it much, much easier in the future to add more things. Great work @skorphil , thank you 🙏 ## Screenshot ## What's Changed * Implemented tabs in settings for better usability by @skorphil in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/75 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.1.4...2.2.0
releases.name: Scribe 2.2.0 Settings Overhaul (Thanks to Skorphil)

Adds a toggle in settings to add your own custom URLs for both the transcription model and language model https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/62 https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/61 https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/60 Thank you so much for implementing this @wobondar and @skorphil for including the Gemini fix ## Screenshot ## What's Changed * Fix issues, reltaed to use of gemini openai-compatible endpoints by @skorphil in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/68 ## New Contributors * @skorphil made their first contribution in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/68 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.1.2...2.1.3 ## What's Changed * feat(llm): add custom OpenAI base URL and model name support by @wobondar in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/61 ## New Contributors * @wobondar made their first contribution in https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/pull/61 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/compare/2.1.1...2.1.2
releases.name: Scribe 2.1.3 - Enables Custom OpenAI URLS + Gemini Fix

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description: Thank you for the feature requests! https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/4 https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/11 # Scribe 2.0 - Custom Templating This has been a long time coming, there was alot of great work to line this up and I'm really glad we landed here! Head on over to the settings in order to create new templates! We now have the ability to add a custom template that gives you _near_ complete control over the output of Scribe, you can completely modify your sections and add new functionality through it. ## What is to come There is more to come here, I am thinking to create the idea of "Profiles", it will be an additional abstraction that encapsulates the Templates, and this will have us be in the space where we can have the language and other more defaulty settings saved in a Profile. This is an MVP of the templating, I think it gets us most of the way there, and I will be likely holding off for user feedback to further with it ## Screenshots
releases.name: Scribe 2.0.0 - Custom Templates

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Scribe 1.1.2 - Minor: Settings Filename Prefix improvements
This is a minor release, with solid implications

## Overhauls the Filename Prefix Settings
This has 2 functions
1. Makes it so the user doesn't have to click the "save settings" button (thank god)
2. Enables us to visibly see the prefix settings in real time
3. Sets us up for https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/11 & https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/4 by creating the first React components for settings.  This is the base work for creating templates through the settings by giving me a nice workspace
releases.name: Scribe 1.1.2 - Minor: Settings Filename Prefix improvements

releases.name: # Scribe 1.1.1 - Feature: enables default options in settings & optionality for saving the audio recording

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Bug Fix
I found a bug where sometimes the model would return `null` for answeredQuestions as opposed to undefined, Zod didn't like that because I didn't specify it as `.nullable()` and instead had it only as `optional()` 

## Setting up custom templates
In order to enable custom templates I needed to refactor how things were appended to notes in order to be more customizable.  This is a full revamp of how we write to notes.
releases.name: Scribe 1.0.9 - 🐞 Bug Fix - improves reliability of parsing LLM output

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Scribe 1.0.8 - Feature MVP Allow user to modify file name prefixes & date format
Closes feature request https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/12
This was a recommendation in this comment https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/11#issuecomment-2596169733

## This is an MVP
This is what I see to be an MVP for this feature.

It's definitely not where I'd like it, and it's as far as it will get for now, is fully functional and provides an improvement over no customization.

## Improvements
- Enables the user to select their transcript note prefix
- Enables the user to select their audio recording filename prefix
- Enables the user to customize their date format - using moment
- Fixes some settings behavior where it was incorrectly showing the directory that was selected

## Future refactor
I am going to rewrite the settings to be in React, I want the prefix to show dynamically and it's just a pain to be working with the raw elements as they are.  The file is becoming unwieldy and annoying to work in.

Once things are in React, which I think I'll tackle when I go to implement the Custom Templates feature 
https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/4 and https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/issues/11

I can make this nice with realtime validation on the date format and some other options.
releases.name: Scribe 1.0.8 - Feature MVP Allow user to modify file name prefixes & date format Latest

releases.author: Mikodin
releases.description:
# Release Notes for Scribe v1.0.0

We are thrilled to announce the first official stable release of Scribe for Obsidian. Below are the detailed steps for installation and a summary of what you can expect from this release.

## Installation Guide

To get started with Scribe, please follow these steps:

1. **Navigate to Your Obsidian Vault:** Find your way to the root of your Obsidian vault.
2. **Create a Folder:** Inside the `plugins` directory of your `.obsidian` folder, create a new folder named `obsidian-scribe`.
3. **Drop the Necessary Files:** Place all the required Scribe plugin files into the `Obsidian Scribe` folder you just created.
4. **Open and Reload Obsidian:** Launch Obsidian, then use the option to reload it, ensuring the new plugin is recognized.
5. **Enable Scribe Plugin:** Head over to the `Community plugins` section, find Scribe, and enable it.
6. **Insert API Keys:** In the Scribe settings within Obsidian, enter your API keys to activate the plugin's functionality.

## What's New

- **Robust Basic Functionality:** While this is quite stable I acknowledge there might be some issues and am committed to resolving them.
- **Community Support:** Encountering problems or have suggestions? I'm all ears. Your feedback is invaluable to me.
releases.name: Scribe 1.0.0
